behavior_summary: GUARDEGG::hatch runs when the GUARD EGG item is used (inferred Event 7).
It checks the egg instance's internal id via Egg::getEggId() and only proceeds when that id equals 1.
When id == 1 the script clears the egg id by calling Egg::setEggId(this, 0).

If the global flag guard1met is true (inferred gate), the script sets the global xavierSitting flag to 1 and forwards the use to the engine item-use processor (process Item::use) with a mode/flag byte (inferred 0x04).
If the egg id is not 1 the function returns immediately without side effects.
notable_control_flow: Early return unless Egg::getEggId(egg_item) == 1 — the id equality check is the primary gate (high confidence).

After clearing the egg id (set to 0), the flow checks the global guard1met flag; only when this flag is set does the script set xavierSitting = 1 and call process Item::use (medium confidence, inferred from conditional jump pattern).
The call to process Item::use runs after state mutation and its return value is stored into a local temp (high confidence).
open_questions: Exact gameplay meaning of Egg id == 1 (appears to represent a specific hatchable state) — confidence: medium.
Precise semantics of the mode/flag byte passed to process Item::use (inferred 0x04) and how it changes item-use processing — confidence: low.
Identity and broader role of 'Xavier' (xavierSitting flag is written but origin/usage is outside the script) — confidence: low.
Whether guard1met must be true for every execution path after id==1 or only for the xavierSitting side-effect (branching pattern inferred but not fully explicit) — confidence: medium.

main_function.name: U8P_GUARDEGG::hatch
main_function.purpose: Handle using the Guard Egg item: if the egg's internal id equals 1, clear that id, optionally update NPC-related game state (xavierSitting) when the guard1met flag is set, and forward the use to the generic item-use processor.
entry_conditions: Event 7 / item-use on the GUARD EGG (inferred).
Egg::getEggId(egg_item) must return 1 for the U8P_GUARDEGG::hatch behavior to run.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARDEGG::hatch
function_map.role: Entry handler for the GUARD EGG's U8P_GUARDEGG::hatch/use event; coordinates state checks, flag updates, and forwarding to item-use processing.

function_map.name: Egg::getEggId
function_map.role: Read the egg instance's internal id/state to determine whether U8P_GUARDEGG::hatch behavior should proceed.

function_map.name: Egg::setEggId
function_map.role: Set/clear the egg instance's internal id/state (used here to set the id to 0).

function_map.name: process Item::use
function_map.role: Invoke the engine's generic item-use processing for the egg after state changes; called with an inferred mode/flag byte.

external_calls.purpose: Query the egg object's internal id/state to decide whether to run U8P_GUARDEGG::hatch behavior (id == 1).
Clear the egg object's id/state by setting it to 0 after detecting id == 1.
Forward the item-use to the engine's generic processor (called after state mutations); invoked with an inferred mode/flag (0x04) and the egg pointer; its return value is stored to a local.

reads.name: guard1met
reads.likely_effect: Used as a gate to determine whether to set xavierSitting and continue to process Item::use; if false the script likely skips those side-effects.

writes.name: xavierSitting
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 to mark the NPC 'Xavier' as sitting (game-state change triggered by using the egg when other conditions are met).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: GUARDEGG::hatch runs when the GUARD EGG item is used (inferred Event 7).
